title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer flags three issues: (1) ConfigMaps created before this PR won't have the new label, so they'll never be found by the label-based pruning logic and will accumulate forever — needs either a documented limitation, prefix-based fallback, or a migration step. (2) No E2E test exists for the new pruning behavior, which is inconsistent with the project's pattern of having Chainsaw E2E coverage for every feature. A third warning was truncated.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 3a5c8bd-dirty with Claude CLI

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=0 warning=3 info=1 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## WARNING (3)

### W-001: Pre-existing ConfigMaps invisible to pruning due to missing labels
**File**: `config.go:229` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Verify blast radius of changes to shared internal code

**Problem**: PruneImmutableConfigMaps uses client.MatchingLabels{ConfigBaseLabelKey: baseName} as a server-side selector, but CreateImmutableConfigMap on main does not set this label — it was added in this same PR. ConfigMaps created before this PR is deployed will lack the forge.c5c3.io/config-base label, so the label-based List will never find them. These pre-existing unlabeled ConfigMaps will accumulate forever and never be pruned.

**Code**:
```
if err := c.List(ctx, &allConfigMaps, client.InNamespace(namespace), client.MatchingLabels{ConfigBaseLabelKey: baseName}); err != nil {
```

**Action Required**: Either (a) document this as a known limitation — only ConfigMaps created after upgrade are prunable — and note that old ones will be GC'd when the owner CR is deleted, or (b) fall back to listing all ConfigMaps and filtering by prefix when the label-based list returns zero candidates, or (c) add a migration step that labels existing ConfigMaps on first reconcile.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Document as a known limitation or implement a migration strategy — requires team decision on which approach to take.
```

---

### W-002: Missing E2E test for ConfigMap pruning
**File**: `tests/e2e/keystone/` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Enforce consistent error-handling pattern across all test cases

**Problem**: The project has 25 Chainsaw E2E test directories under tests/e2e/keystone/ covering every existing feature. This PR adds a new operational behavior (pruning) but includes no tests/e2e/keystone/config-pruning/ directory. Unit and integration tests are thorough, but an E2E test that deploys a Keystone CR, triggers multiple config changes, and asserts the ConfigMap count after pruning would verify the end-to-end wiring on a real cluster.

**Code**:
```
tests/e2e/keystone/ (25 existing Chainsaw test directories — no config-pruning directory present)
```

**Action Required**: Create tests/e2e/keystone/config-pruning/chainsaw-test.yaml that: (1) deploys a Keystone CR, (2) patches the spec to trigger config changes multiple times, (3) waits for DeploymentReady, (4) asserts the number of ConfigMaps matching the prefix is at most retain + 1.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Create tests/e2e/keystone/config-pruning/chainsaw-test.yaml covering deploy, multiple config changes, and post-pruning ConfigMap count assertion.
```

**Related**: W-003

---

### W-003: Integration test does not verify which ConfigMaps were retained
**File**: `integration_test.go:179-180` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Assert absence of old state in mutation tests

**Problem**: TestIntegration_PruneImmutableConfigMaps asserts remaining has length 4 and contains currentName, but does not assert that the oldest ConfigMap was deleted. Since CreateImmutableConfigMap is called in a loop with hash-based names, the test cannot distinguish 'retained the 3 newest' from 'retained 3 random ones.'

**Code**:
```
g.Expect(remaining).To(HaveLen(4), "current + 3 retained historical ConfigMaps expected")
g.Expect(remaining).To(ContainElement(currentName), "current ConfigMap must survive pruning")
```

**Action Required**: Record the first ConfigMap name created in the loop and add an assertion that it is absent from the remaining list to verify the oldest was actually pruned.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
g.Expect(remaining).To(HaveLen(4), "current + 3 retained historical ConfigMaps expected")
g.Expect(remaining).To(ContainElement(currentName), "current ConfigMap must survive pruning")
g.Expect(remaining).NotTo(ContainElement(firstCreatedName), "oldest ConfigMap should have been pruned")
```

**Related**: W-002

---

## INFO (1)

### I-001: Delete call missing PropagationPolicy diverges from codebase convention
**File**: `config.go:260` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: likely — **Pattern**: Check resource deletion for orphan cleanup policy

**Problem**: The codebase has an established pattern of using DeletePropagationBackground for delete operations (in job.go:82 and reconcile_policyvalidation.go:204). The pruning delete at config.go:260 uses a bare c.Delete(ctx, &cm) without specifying propagation policy, diverging from the established convention.

**Code**:
```
if err := client.IgnoreNotFound(c.Delete(ctx, &cm)); err != nil {
```

**Action Required**: No change required — ConfigMaps have no owned children so propagation policy is irrelevant. Only change if project style mandates uniform propagation on all deletes.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
No change needed. ConfigMaps have no owned children, so propagation policy is functionally irrelevant here.
```

---

## Summary

This is a well-implemented PR with clean architecture: pruning logic in the shared library with thorough unit tests, a thin controller wrapper, and comprehensive documentation. Test coverage is excellent with 12 unit tests, 2 integration tests, and 3 controller-level tests. The most significant concern is that pre-existing ConfigMaps created before this PR will never be pruned because they lack the new label used as a server-side selector. The absence of an E2E/Chainsaw test is notable given the project's otherwise complete E2E coverage for every feature.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 0 |
| WARNING  | 3 |
| INFO     | 1 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Merge after addressing the label-based selector limitation (CRITICAL-1) — either document it as a known limitation or implement a migration strategy. The missing E2E test (WARNING-1) and integration test assertion gap (WARNING-2) should ideally be addressed before merge but are not blocking given the strong unit test coverage.
resolution_summary: All four review comments were addressed. W-001 (pre-existing ConfigMaps invisible to pruning) was resolved by documenting it as a known limitation in the PruneImmutableConfigMaps godoc (config.go:221-226), noting that pre-existing unlabeled ConfigMaps are bounded and will be garbage-collected when the owner CR is deleted. W-002 (missing E2E test) was resolved by creating tests/e2e/keystone/config-pruning/chainsaw-test.yaml with a 00-keystone-cr.yaml fixture, covering CR deploy, 4 sequential config patches, DeploymentReady waits, and a final assertion that ConfigMap count is at most retain+1=4. W-003 (integration test not verifying which ConfigMaps were retained) was addressed at the unit test level rather than the integration test: TestPruneImmutableConfigMaps_deletesStaleConfigMaps in config_test.go explicitly asserts absence of the two oldest ConfigMaps (NotTo ContainElement), and the controller-level test TestPruneStaleConfigMaps_deletesOldConfigMaps verifies deletion via apierrors.IsNotFound; the envtest integration test was not modified to add absence assertions because hash-based names from CreateImmutableConfigMap are not deterministic. I-001 (missing PropagationPolicy on delete) required no change per the reviewer's own assessment, and none was made.
